# locuterra - Civic Social Network Concept and Demonstrator

> LOCUTERRA is a fully specified, open-source concept for a public-interest, location-based social network. It is freely donated for municipalities, non-profit organisations, and public-sector institutions to build upon. The repository includes a Next.js/React/TypeScript demonstrator with a fictional municipality ("Grüntal") and synthetic data.

Part of the Um:bruch civic tech family. MIT.

## Docs

- [README.md](https://github.com/um-bruch/locuterra/blob/master/README.md): English overview, demonstrator setup, and concept document index.
- [README_de.md](https://github.com/um-bruch/locuterra/blob/master/README_de.md): German localization.
- [KONZEPT.md](https://github.com/um-bruch/locuterra/blob/master/KONZEPT.md): Original product idea and MVP scope.
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](https://github.com/um-bruch/locuterra/blob/master/ARCHITECTURE.md): Technical target stack and domain modules.
- [GOVERNANCE.md](https://github.com/um-bruch/locuterra/blob/master/GOVERNANCE.md): Stewardship, moderation, and appeals model.
- [DATENSCHUTZ.md](https://github.com/um-bruch/locuterra/blob/master/DATENSCHUTZ.md): Data protection and consent model.
- [ROLES_AND_RIGHTS.md](https://github.com/um-bruch/locuterra/blob/master/ROLES_AND_RIGHTS.md): Actor, role, and permissions model.
- [FINANZIERUNGSKONZEPT.md](https://github.com/um-bruch/locuterra/blob/master/FINANZIERUNGSKONZEPT.md): Arena model, sponsoring, and revenue sharing.
- [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/um-bruch/locuterra/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md): Project version history.
- [LICENSE](https://github.com/um-bruch/locuterra/blob/master/LICENSE): MIT license text.

## Demonstrator

Live demo: https://um-bruch.github.io/locuterra/
Demo preview image: https://um-bruch.github.io/locuterra/demo-preview.png

Built with Next.js, React, and TypeScript. Shows fictional municipality "Grüntal" with synthetic data: 9 places, 5 groups, 8 resources, 2 information channels, direct messages. Statically exportable, no backend required.

## Audience

- Municipalities and public-sector institutions seeking a civic social network blueprint
- Non-profit organisations building local community digital infrastructure
- Civic tech developers and researchers studying location-based social networks
- Open-source contributors interested in PWA-first community platform design
- Policy makers and digital commons advocates exploring participatory local platforms

- locuterra civic social network open source concept
- location-based community platform municipalities blueprint

- public interest social network GDPR compliant open concept
